verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचअथ विज्ञाय भगवान् सर्वात्मा सर्वदर्शन: ।सैरन्ध्र‍या: कामतप्ताया: प्रियमिच्छन् गृहं ययौ ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaatha vijñāya bhagavānsarvātmā sarva-darśanaḥsairandhryāḥ kāma-taptāyāḥpriyam icchan gṛhaṁ yayau
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Next, after assimilating Uddhava’s report, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the omniscient Soul of all that be, desired to satisfy the serving girl Trivakrā, who was troubled by lust. Thus He went to her house.

verses.sanskrit: महार्होपस्करैराढ्यं कामोपायोपबृंहितम् ।मुक्तादामपताकाभिर्वितानशयनासनै: ।धूपै: सुरभिभिर्दीपै: स्रग्गन्धैरपि मण्डितम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: mahārhopaskarair āḍhyaṁkāmopāyopabṛṁhitammuktā-dāma-patākābhirvitāna-śayanāsanaiḥdhūpaiḥ surabhibhir dīpaiḥsrag-gandhair api maṇḍitam
verses.text: Trivakrā’s home was opulently appointed with expensive furnishings and replete with sensual accoutrements meant to inspire sexual desire. There were banners, rows of strung pearls, canopies, fine beds and sitting places, and also fragrant incense, oil lamps, flower garlands and aromatic sandalwood paste.

verses.sanskrit: गृहं तमायान्तमवेक्ष्य सासनात्सद्य: समुत्थाय हि जातसम्भ्रमा ।यथोपसङ्गम्य सखीभिरच्युतंसभाजयामास सदासनादिभि: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: gṛhaṁ tam āyāntam avekṣya sāsanātsadyaḥ samutthāya hi jāta-sambhramāyathopasaṅgamya sakhībhir acyutaṁsabhājayām āsa sad-āsanādibhiḥ
verses.text: When Trivakrā saw Him arriving at her house, she at once rose from her seat in a flurry. Coming forward graciously with her girlfriends, she respectfully greeted Lord Acyuta by offering Him an excellent seat and other articles of worship.

verses.sanskrit: तथोद्धव: साधु तयाभिपूजितोन्यषीददुर्व्यामभिमृश्य चासनम् ।कृष्णोऽपि तूर्णं शयनं महाधनंविवेश लोकाचरितान्यनुव्रत: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathoddhavaḥ sādhutayābhipūjitonyaṣīdad urvyām abhimṛśya cāsanamkṛṣṇo ’pi tūrṇaṁ śayanaṁ mahā-dhanaṁviveśa lokācaritāny anuvrataḥ
verses.text: Uddhava also received a seat of honor, since he was a saintly person, but he simply touched it and sat on the floor. Then Lord Kṛṣṇa, imitating the manners of human society, quickly made Himself comfortable on an opulent bed.

verses.sanskrit: सा मज्जनालेपदुकूलभूषण-स्रग्गन्धताम्बूलसुधासवादिभि: ।प्रसाधितात्मोपससार माधवंसव्रीडलीलोत्स्मितविभ्रमेक्षितै: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā majjanālepa-dukūla-bhūṣaṇasrag-gandha-tāmbūla-sudhāsavādibhiḥprasādhitātmopasasāra mādhavaṁsa-vrīḍa-līlotsmita-vibhramekṣitaiḥ
verses.text: Trivakrā prepared herself by bathing, anointing her body, and dressing in fine garments, by putting on jewelry, garlands and perfume, and also by chewing betel nut, drinking fragrant liquor, and so on. She then approached Lord Mādhava with shy, playful smiles and coquettish glances.

verses.sanskrit: आहूय कान्तां नवसङ्गमह्रियाविशङ्कितां कङ्कणभूषिते करे ।प्रगृह्य शय्यामधिवेश्य रामयारेमेऽनुलेपार्पणपुण्यलेशया ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: āhūya kāntāṁ nava-saṅgama-hriyāviśaṅkitāṁ kaṅkaṇa-bhūṣite karepragṛhya śayyām adhiveśya rāmayāreme ’nulepārpaṇa-puṇya-leśayā
verses.text: Calling forward His beloved, who was anxious and shy at the prospect of this new contact, the Lord pulled her by her bangled hands onto the bed. Thus He enjoyed with that beautiful girl, whose only trace of piety was her having offered ointment to the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: सानङ्गतप्तकुचयोरुरसस्तथाक्ष्णो-र्जिघ्रन्त्यनन्तचरणेन रुजो मृजन्ती ।दोर्भ्यां स्तनान्तरगतं परिरभ्य कान्त-मानन्दमूर्तिमजहादतिदीर्घतापम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sānaṅga-tapta-kucayor urasas tathākṣṇorjighranty ananta-caraṇena rujo mṛjantīdorbhyāṁ stanāntara-gataṁ parirabhya kāntamānanda-mūrtim ajahād ati-dīrgha-tāpam
verses.text: Simply by smelling the fragrance of Kṛṣṇa’s lotus feet, Trivakrā cleansed away the burning lust Cupid had aroused in her breasts, chest and eyes. With her two arms she embraced between her breasts her lover, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the personification of bliss, and thus she gave up her long-standing distress.

verses.sanskrit: सैवं कैवल्यनाथं तं प्राप्य दुष्प्राप्यमीश्वरम् ।अङ्गरागार्पणेनाहो दुर्भगेदमयाचत ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: saivaṁ kaivalya-nāthaṁ taṁprāpya duṣprāpyam īśvaramaṅga-rāgārpaṇenāhodurbhagedam ayācata
verses.text: Having thus gotten the hard-to-get Supreme Lord by the simple act of offering Him body ointment, unfortunate Trivakrā submitted to that Lord of freedom the following request.

verses.sanskrit: सहोष्यतामिह प्रेष्ठ दिनानि कतिचिन्मया ।रमस्व नोत्सहे त्यक्तुं सङ्गं तेऽम्बुरुहेक्षण ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sahoṣyatām iha preṣṭhadināni katicin mayāramasva notsahe tyaktuṁsaṅgaṁ te ’mburuhekṣaṇa
verses.text: [Trivakrā said:] O beloved, please stay here with me for a few days more and enjoy. I cannot bear to give up Your association, O lotus-eyed one!

verses.sanskrit: तस्यै कामवरं दत्त्वा मानयित्वा च मानद: ।सहोद्धवेन सर्वेश: स्वधामागमद् ऋद्धिमत् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyai kāma-varaṁ dattvāmānayitvā ca māna-daḥsahoddhavena sarveśaḥsva-dhāmāgamad ṛddhimat
verses.text: Promising her the fulfillment of this lusty desire, considerate Kṛṣṇa, Lord of all beings, paid Trivakrā His respects and then returned with Uddhava to His own supremely opulent residence.

verses.sanskrit: दुराराध्यं समाराध्य विष्णुं सर्वेश्वरेश्वरम् ।यो वृणीते मनोग्राह्यमसत्त्वात् कुमनीष्यसौ ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: durārdhyaṁ samārādhyaviṣṇuṁ sarveśvareśvaramyo vṛṇīte mano-grāhyamasattvāt kumanīṣy asau
verses.text: Lord Viṣṇu, the Supreme Lord of all lords, is ordinarily difficult to approach. One who has properly worshiped Him and then chooses the benediction of mundane sense gratification is certainly of poor intelligence, for he is satisfied with an insignificant result.

verses.sanskrit: अक्रूरभवनं कृष्ण: सहरामोद्धव: प्रभु: ।किञ्चिच्चिकीर्षयन् प्रागादक्रूरप्रीयकाम्यया ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: akrūra-bhavanaṁ kṛṣṇaḥsaha-rāmoddhavaḥ prabhuḥkiñcic cikīrṣayan prāgādakrūra-priya-kāmyayā
verses.text: Then Lord Kṛṣṇa, wanting to have some things done, went to Akrūra’s house with Balarāma and Uddhava. The Lord also desired to please Akrūra.

verses.sanskrit: स तान्नरवरश्रेष्ठानाराद् वीक्ष्य स्वबान्धवान् ।प्रत्युत्थाय प्रमुदित: परिष्वज्याभिनन्द्य च ॥ १३ ॥ननाम कृष्णं रामं च स तैरप्यभिवादित: ।पूजयामास विधिवत् कृतासनपरिग्रहान् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa tān nara-vara-śreṣṭhānārād vīkṣya sva-bāndhavānpratyutthāya pramuditaḥpariṣvajyābhinandya ca
verses.text: Akrūra stood up in great joy when he saw them, his own relatives and the greatest of exalted personalities, coming from a distance. After embracing them and greeting them, Akrūra bowed down to Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma and was greeted by Them in return. Then, when his guests had taken their seats, he worshiped them in accordance with scriptural rules.

verses.sanskrit: पादावनेजनीरापो धारयन् शिरसा नृप ।अर्हणेनाम्बरैर्दिव्यैर्गन्धस्रग्भूषणोत्तमै: ॥ १५ ॥अर्चित्वा शिरसानम्य पादावङ्कगतौ मृजन् ।प्रश्रयावनतोऽक्रूर: कृष्णरामावभाषत ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: pādāvanejanīr āpodhārayan śirasā nṛpaarhaṇenāmbarair divyairgandha-srag-bhūṣaṇottamaiḥ
verses.text: O King, Akrūra bathed the feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa and Lord Balarāma and then poured the bath water on his head. He presented Them with gifts of fine clothing, aromatic sandalwood paste, flower garlands and excellent jewelry. After thus worshiping the two Lords, he bowed his head to the floor. He then began to massage Lord Kṛṣṇa’s feet, placing them on his lap, and with his head bowed in humility he addressed Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma as follows.

verses.sanskrit: दिष्‍ट्या पापो हत: कंस: सानुगो वामिदं कुलम् ।भवद्भ‍य‍ामुद्‍धृतं कृच्छ्राद् दुरन्ताच्च समेधितम् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: diṣṭyā pāpo hataḥ kaṁsaḥsānugo vām idaṁ kulambhavadbhyām uddhṛtaṁ kṛcchrāddurantāc ca samedhitam
verses.text: [Akrūra said:] It is our good fortune that You two Lords have killed the evil Kaṁsa and his followers, thus delivering Your dynasty from endless suffering and causing it to flourish.

verses.sanskrit: युवां प्रधानपुरुषौ जगद्धेतू जगन्मयौ ।भवद्भ‍य‍ां न विना किञ्चित्परमस्ति न चापरम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: yuvāṁ pradhāna-puruṣaujagad-dhetū jagan-mayaubhavadbhyāṁ na vinā kiñcitparam asti na cāparam
verses.text: You both are the original Supreme Person, the cause of the universe and its very substance. Not the slightest subtle cause or manifest product of creation exists apart from You.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मसृष्टमिदं विश्वमन्वाविश्य स्वशक्तिभि: ।ईयते बहुधा ब्रह्मन् श्रुतप्रत्यक्षगोचरम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: ātma-sṛṣṭam idaṁ viśvamanvāviśya sva-śaktibhiḥīyate bahudhā brahmanśruta-pratyakṣa-gocaram
verses.text: O Supreme Absolute Truth, with Your personal energies You create this universe and then enter into it. Thus one can perceive You in many different forms by hearing from authorities and by direct experience.

verses.sanskrit: यथा हि भूतेषु चराचरेषुमह्यादयो योनिषु भान्ति नाना ।एवं भवान् केवल आत्मयोनि-ष्वात्मात्मतन्त्रो बहुधा विभाति ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā hi bhūteṣu carācareṣumahy-ādayo yoniṣu bhānti nānāevaṁ bhavān kevala ātma-yoniṣvātmātma-tantro bahudhā vibhāti
verses.text: Just as the primary elements — earth and so on — manifest themselves in abundant variety among all the species of mobile and immobile life, so You, the one independent Supreme Soul, appear to be manifold among the variegated objects of Your creation.

verses.sanskrit: सृजस्यथो लुम्पसि पासि विश्वंरजस्तम:सत्त्वगुणै: स्वशक्तिभि: ।न बध्यसे तद्गुणकर्मभिर्वाज्ञानात्मनस्ते क्व‍ च बन्धहेतु: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sṛjasy atho lumpasi pāsi viśvaṁrajas-tamaḥ-sattva-guṇaiḥ sva-śaktibhiḥna badhyase tad-guṇa-karmabhir vājñānātmanas te kva ca bandha-hetuḥ
verses.text: You create, destroy and also maintain this universe with Your personal energies — the modes of passion, ignorance and goodness — yet You are never entangled by these modes or the activities they generate. Since You are the original source of all knowledge, what could ever cause You to be bound by illusion?

verses.sanskrit: देहाद्युपाधेरनिरूपितत्वाद्भवो न साक्षान्न भिदात्मन: स्यात् ।अतो न बन्धस्तव नैव मोक्ष:स्यातां निकामस्त्वयि नोऽविवेक: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: dehādy-upādher anirūpitatvādbhavo na sākṣān na bhidātmanaḥ syātato na bandhas tava naiva mokṣaḥsyātām nikāmas tvayi no ’vivekaḥ
verses.text: Since it has never been demonstrated that You are covered by material, bodily designations, it must be concluded that for You there is neither birth in a literal sense nor any duality. Therefore You never undergo bondage or liberation, and if You appear to, it is only because of Your desire that we see You in that way, or simply because of our lack of discrimination.

verses.sanskrit: त्वयोदितोऽयं जगतो हिताययदा यदा वेदपथ: पुराण: ।बाध्येत पाषण्डपथैरसद्भ‍ि-स्तदा भवान् सत्त्वगुणं बिभर्ति ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvayodito ’yaṁ jagato hitāyayadā yadā veda-pathaḥ purāṇaḥbādhyeta pāṣaṇḍa-pathair asadbhistadā bhavān sattva-guṇaṁ bibharti
verses.text: You originally enunciated the ancient religious path of the Vedas for the benefit of the whole universe. Whenever that path becomes obstructed by wicked persons following the path of atheism, You assume one of Your incarnations, which are all in the transcendental mode of goodness.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं प्रभोऽद्य वसुदेवगृहेऽवतीर्ण:स्वांशेन भारमपनेतुमिहासि भूमे: ।अक्षौहिणीशतवधेन सुरेतरांश-राज्ञाममुष्य च कुलस्य यशो वितन्वन् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa tvam prabho ’dya vasudeva-gṛhe ’vatīrṇaḥsvāṁśena bhāram apanetum ihāsi bhūmeḥakṣauhiṇī-śata-vadhena suretarāṁśa-rājñām amuṣya ca kulasya yaśo vitanvan
verses.text: You are that very same Supreme Person, my Lord, and You have now appeared in the home of Vasudeva with Your plenary portion. You have done this to relieve the earth’s burden by killing hundreds of armies led by kings who are expansions of the demigods’ enemies, and also to spread the fame of our dynasty.

verses.sanskrit: अद्येश नो वसतय: खलु भूरिभागाय: सर्वदेवपितृभूतनृदेवमूर्ति: ।यत्पादशौचसलिलं त्रिजगत् पुनातिस त्वं जगद्गुरुरधोक्षज या: प्रविष्ट: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: adyeśa no vasatayaḥ khalu bhūri-bhāgāyaḥ sarva-deva-pitṛ-bhūta-nṛ-deva-mūrtiḥyat-pāda-śauca-salilaṁ tri-jagat punātisa tvaṁ jagad-gurur adhokṣaja yāḥ praviṣṭaḥ
verses.text: Today, O Lord, my home has become most fortunate because You have entered it. As the Supreme Truth, You embody the forefathers, ordinary creatures, human beings and demigods, and the water that has washed Your feet purifies the three worlds. Indeed, O transcendent one, You are the spiritual master of the universe.

verses.sanskrit: क: पण्डितस्त्वदपरं शरणं समीयाद्भक्तप्रियाद‍ृतगिर: सुहृद: कृतज्ञात् ।सर्वान् ददाति सुहृदो भजतोऽभिकामा-नात्मानमप्युपचयापचयौ न यस्य ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaḥ paṇḍitas tvad aparaṁ śaraṇaṁ samīyādbhakta-priyād ṛta-giraḥ suhṛdaḥ kṛta-jñātsarvān dadāti suhṛdo bhajato ’bhikāmānātmānam apy upacayāpacayau na yasya
verses.text: What learned person would approach anyone but You for shelter, when You are the affectionate, grateful and truthful well-wisher of Your devotees? To those who worship You in sincere friendship You reward everything they desire, even Your own self, yet You never increase or diminish.

verses.sanskrit: दिष्‍ट्या जनार्दन भवानिह न: प्रतीतोयोगेश्वरैरपि दुरापगति: सुरेशै: ।छिन्ध्याशु न: सुतकलत्रधनाप्तगेह-देहादिमोहरशनां भवदीयमायाम् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: diṣṭyā janārdana bhavān iha naḥ pratītoyogeśvarair api durāpa-gatiḥ sureśaiḥchindhy āśu naḥ suta-kalatra-dhanāpta-geha-dehādi-moha-raśanāṁ bhavadīya-māyām
verses.text: It is by our great fortune, Janārdana, that You are now visible to us, for even the masters of yoga and the foremost demigods can achieve this goal only with great difficulty. Please quickly cut the ropes of our illusory attachment for children, wife, wealth, influential friends, home and body. All such attachment is simply the effect of Your illusory material energy.

verses.sanskrit: इत्यर्चित: संस्तुतश्च भक्तेन भगवान् हरि: ।अक्रूरं सस्मितं प्राह गीर्भि: सम्मोहयन्निव ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity arcitaḥ saṁstutaś cabhaktena bhagavān hariḥakrūraṁ sa-smitaṁ prāhagīrbhiḥ sammohayann iva
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Thus worshiped and fully glorified by His devotee, the Supreme Lord Hari smilingly addressed Akrūra, completely charming him with His words.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचत्वं नो गुरु: पितृव्यश्च श्लाघ्यो बन्धुश्च नित्यदा ।वयं तु रक्ष्या: पोष्याश्च अनुकम्प्या: प्रजा हि व: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācatvaṁ no guruḥ pitṛvyaś caślāghyo bandhuś ca nityadāvayaṁ tu rakṣyāḥ poṣyāś caanukampyāḥ prajā hi vaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Lord said: You are Our spiritual master, paternal uncle and praiseworthy friend, and We are like your sons, always dependent on your protection, sustenance and compassion.

verses.sanskrit: भवद्विधा महाभागा निषेव्या अर्हसत्तमा: ।श्रेयस्कामैर्नृभिर्नित्यं देवा: स्वार्था न साधव: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: bhavad-vidhā mahā-bhāgāniṣevyā arha-sattamāḥśreyas-kāmair nṛbhir nityaṁdevāḥ svārthā na sādhavaḥ
verses.text: Exalted souls like you are the true objects of service and the most worshipable authorities for those who desire the highest good in life. Demigods are generally concerned with their own interests, but saintly devotees never are.

verses.sanskrit: न ह्यम्मयानि तीर्थानि न देवा मृच्छिलामया: ।ते पुनन्त्युरुकालेन दर्शनादेव साधव: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hy am-mayāni tīrthānina devā mṛc-chilā-mayāḥte punanty uru-kālenadarśanād eva sādhavaḥ
verses.text: No one can deny that there are holy places with sacred rivers, or that the demigods appear in deity forms made of earth and stone. But these purify the soul only after a long time, whereas saintly persons purify just by being seen.

verses.sanskrit: स भवान् सुहृदां वै न: श्रेयान् श्रेयश्चिकीर्षया ।जिज्ञासार्थं पाण्डवानां गच्छस्व त्वं गजाह्वयम् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa bhavān suhṛdāṁ vai naḥśreyān śreyaś-cikīrṣayājijñāsārthaṁ pāṇḍavānāṁgacchasva tvaṁ gajāhvayam
verses.text: You are indeed the best of Our friends, so please go to Hastināpura and, as the well-wisher of the Pāṇḍavas, find out how they are doing.

verses.sanskrit: पितर्युपरते बाला: सह मात्रा सुदु:खिता: ।आनीता: स्वपुरं राज्ञा वसन्त इति शुश्रुम ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitary uparate bālāḥsaha mātrā su-duḥkhitāḥānītāḥ sva-puraṁ rājñāvasanta iti śuśruma
verses.text: We have heard that when their father passed away, the young Pāṇḍavas were brought with their anguished mother to the capital city by King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, and that they are now living there.

verses.sanskrit: तेषु राजाम्बिकापुत्रो भ्रातृपुत्रेषु दीनधी: ।समो न वर्तते नूनं दुष्पुत्रवशगोऽन्धद‍ृक् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: teṣu rājāmbikā-putrobhrātṛ-putreṣu dīna-dhīḥsamo na vartate nūnaṁduṣputra-vaśa-go ’ndha-dṛk
verses.text: Indeed, weak-minded Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, has come under the control of his wicked sons, and therefore that blind King is not treating his brother’s sons fairly.

verses.sanskrit: गच्छ जानीहि तद् वृत्तमधुना साध्वसाधु वा ।विज्ञाय तद् विधास्यामो यथा शं सुहृदां भवेत् ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: gaccha jānīhi tad-vṛttamadhunā sādhv asādhu vāvijñāya tad vidhāsyāmoyathā śaṁ suhṛdāṁ bhavet
verses.text: Go and see whether Dhṛtarāṣṭra is acting properly or not. When We find out, We will make the necessary arrangements to help Our dear friends.

verses.sanskrit: इत्यक्रूरं समादिश्य भगवान् हरिरीश्वर: ।सङ्कर्षणोद्धवाभ्यां वै तत: स्वभवनं ययौ ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity akrūraṁ samādiśyabhagavān harir īśvaraḥsaṅkarṣaṇoddhavābhyāṁ vaitataḥ sva-bhavanaṁ yayau
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Thus fully instructing Akrūra, the Supreme Personality of Godhead Hari then returned to His residence, accompanied by Lord Saṅkarṣaṇa and Uddhava.
